case.title: BENDIX CORPORATION v. RADIO POSITION FINDING CORP.

opinion.h4: BENDIX CORPORATION v. RADIO POSITION FINDING CORP., (1963)
BENDIX CORPORATION v. RADIO POSITION FINDING CORP. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE DISTRICT OF MARYLAND. No. 645. Decided January 21, 1963.
opinion.p: Edward S. Irons and Harold J. Brich for appellant.
MISSOURI EX REL. JOHNSON, ADMINISTRATRIX, ET AL. v. CLAY, SUPERINTENDENT OF DIVISION OF INSURANCE, ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF MISSOURI. No. 607. Decided January 21, 1963.

Thomas F. Eagleton, Attorney General of Missouri, Harry H. Kay, Special Assistant Attorney General, and John C. Baumann for Clay, and James M. Douglas and William G. Guerri for Aetna Insurance Co., appellees.
The motions to dismiss are granted and the appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
